highlights.summary: Move profile creation before autosens in APSManager startup. The patch (Trio/Sources/APS/APSManager.swift) removes a small amount of previous parallelism (async let) and instead calls openAPS.createProfiles(useSwiftOref:), then awaits fetchCurrentTempBasal and autosense sequentially, and passes the fetched currentTemp into openAPS.determineBasal. (See diff and commits.)
highlights.userFacingImpact: This change addresses the Swift autosens assumption that a profile object exists at startup: by creating profiles before calling autosense it is expected to prevent the autosens exception reported for systems started with oref-swift and allow the algorithm to make progress on cold start. The patch description and diff state this intent, but runtime verification is recommended—please run/validate cold-start behavior in your environment.

items.summary: PR and commits state a behavioral fix: change manual temp basal checking so it is performed for any PM instead of only for OmnipodKit (pr:gordolio/Trio#1218; pr:nightscout/Trio#1218; commit:713f9912dfa4; commit:82bd6236cf7f). The change set lists modifications to Trio/Sources/APS/DeviceDataManager.swift and Trio/Sources/Helpers/BasalDeliveryState+Extension.swift (file:456babda339b; file:77e4c7867744), and includes extensive Xcode project file edits (Trio.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj, file:65b106e402b8). The PR author also notes uncertainty about the large project-file changes (pr:gordolio/Trio#1218). Diff metadata reflects the merge commit and project-file adjustments (diff:8c8dd5e01b51).
items.userFacingImpact: Intended to make manual temp-basal checks apply to any PM (broader than prior OmnipodKit-only behavior) if the code changes were applied as described (pr:gordolio/Trio#1218; commit:713f9912dfa4). The supplied diff and files list show project-file edits and that DeviceDataManager.swift and BasalDeliveryState+Extension.swift were changed (file:65b106e402b8; file:456babda339b; file:77e4c7867744), but the truncated diff does not show the precise Swift-code edits. Because the actual Swift-code diffs are not present in the supplied diff, a human review is required to confirm the exact behavioral impact and that no other side effects were introduced.

The patch (Trio/Sources/APS/APSManager.swift) removes a small amount of previous parallelism (async let) and instead calls openAPS.createProfiles(useSwiftOref:), then awaits fetchCurrentTempBasal and autosense sequentially, and passes the fetched currentTemp into openAPS.determineBasal. (See diff and commits.)
items.userFacingImpact: This change addresses the Swift autosens assumption that a profile object exists at startup: by creating profiles before calling autosense it is expected to prevent the autosens exception reported for systems started with oref-swift and allow the algorithm to make progress on cold start. The patch description and diff state this intent, but runtime verification is recommended—please run/validate cold-start behavior in your environment.

items.summary: Bump APP_DEV_VERSION in Config.xcconfig across multiple commits: APP_DEV_VERSION 0.8.3.4 → 0.8.3.5 (commit:ed949dc15636090da343cd0d3fb9bd7452bbe3a4); APP_DEV_VERSION 0.8.3.5 → 0.8.3.6 (commit:b5e07cee1a80d0d64864a9d0025249ace4c64ce8); APP_DEV_VERSION 0.8.3.6 → 0.8.3.7 (commit:179c86398d86a50ec13a78493c733fbf8270319c).
items.userFacingImpact: These commits update development/build version metadata (APP_DEV_VERSION) in Config.xcconfig. The changes are metadata-only and do not show code or runtime behavior changes in the supplied diffs; they primarily affect build/version identifiers. Human review is recommended if confirmation of CI/release effects is required.

items.summary: Update MedtrumKit submodule to a newer commit (subproject commit changed to 1c2d10fd73478f37773f96facf30ff63dbbc05e1) and incorporate upstream MedtrumKit changes. The upstream changelog referenced in the PR descriptions lists: a navigation title fix; introduction of LoopKit alerts; prevention of patch deactivation during bolus; and BLE threading fixes. The repository diff shows the submodule pointer change and the commits record the chore: update MedtrumKit update.
items.userFacingImpact: This PR updates the MedtrumKit submodule only. The supplied materials (PR descriptions) indicate the upstream MedtrumKit includes UI and behavior changes (navigation title fix, LoopKit alerts, prevention of patch deactivation during bolus) and BLE threading fixes, but the provided change in this repository is a submodule pointer update and does not show which of those upstream changes (if any) are exercised by Trio. Evidence is insufficient to determine concrete runtime or user-visible effects within Trio — human review required to assess impact and testing needed to verify behavior.

includedPullRequests.title: fix: Always check manual temp basal with any PM
includedPullRequests.body:
Currently, only manual temp basals will be checked with OmnipodKit. This PR changes this to any PM in preparation of issue: https://github.com/jbr7rr/MedtrumKit/issues/135

Not sure why XCode made this many changes to the project file

includedPullRequests.title: Make sure that autosens has a profile to work with
includedPullRequests.body:
The Swift version of autosens expects to have a profile object, so if
you start with oref-swift from the start the current version will
throw an exception on autosens since there is no profile, and the
algorithm will never make progress.

This change swaps the order of autosens and profile creation. Profile
creation doesn't depend on autosens, so this change is safe and
enables systems set up as oref-swift from the start run.

Note: There was a tiny amount of parallelism in the previous version,
but not enough to justify so I removed it to make the code more
straightforward.

Thanks to @mountrcg for reporting this originally.

you start with oref-swift from the start the current version will
throw an exception on autosens since there is no profile, and the
algorithm will never make progress.

This change swaps the order of autosens and profile creation. Profile
creation doesn't depend on autosens, so this change is safe and
enables systems set up as oref-swift from the start run.

Note: There was a tiny amount of parallelism in the previous version,
but not enough to justify so I removed it to make the code more
straightforward.

Thanks to @mountrcg for reporting this originally.
